entities.description: rockchip developer guide pcle cn is a document that provides developer guidance for pcie technology  specifically mentioning sections about controller initialization issues
pcle3 0    is a technology component that requires specific initialization procedures to prevent system anomalies
pcle 2 0    is a technology component that requires specific initialization procedures to prevent system anomalies
linux    is the operating system kernel that needs to be configured to disable pcie functionality when not in use

relations.description: the document discusses the initialization of pcle3 0    and its potential to cause system anomalies if not properly configured
the document discusses the initialization of pcle 2 0    and its potential to cause system anomalies if not properly configured
the pcle3 0    requires the linux    to be configured to disable pcie functionality when not in use to prevent system anomalies
the pcle 2 0    requires the linux    to be configured to disable pcie functionality when not in use to prevent system anomalies
the linux    uses       to describe hardware components and configure pcie functionality
the pcle3 0    may cause system   if pcie functionality is not properly disabled in the linux
the pcle 2 0    may cause system   if pcie functionality is not properly disabled in the linux
